value.found_quote: Prompt injection from untrusted web content... Prompt injection can happen when the agent retrieves and follows instructions from untrusted content (for example, a web page or dependency README).
value.rationale: The page explicitly acknowledges prompt injection as a risk when agent internet access is enabled and describes defenses against it: blocking internet access by default, allowing domain allowlists, restricting HTTP methods (GET/HEAD/OPTIONS only), and advising users to point Codex only to trusted resources. These are described as active mitigations/controls against malicious instructions in web content, which substantiates the claim of OpenAI offering prompt-injection protection in the context of Codex cloud agents.
